channel.title: Misc on MarkJacobsen.net
channel.description: Recent content in Misc on MarkJacobsen.net

item.title: Why pay for a “rare” handle when you can get one for free?
item.description:
$2500 as the lowest price? What a joke. Seriously, setup your own Mastodon instance and create as many handles as you want, or join just about any number of Mastodon instances.

X to launch marketplace for buying inactive handles

item.title: State gun control laws reduce children’s firearm deaths, study shows
item.description:
Check out this article: [State gun control laws reduce children’s firearm deaths, study shows][1]

“If more guns made us safer, the US would be the safest country in the world by far. Instead, we have a homicide rate that’s 25 times that of our peer nations.”

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there is no commentary on it other than any minimal notes I may have included. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some/more of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not. Should the article be behind a paywall let me know and I will figure out a way to get it to you.[1]: https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/jun/20/state-gun-control-laws-children-deaths-study

item.title: Systems are crumbling – but daily life continues. The dissonance is real
item.description:
Check out this article: [Systems are crumbling – but daily life continues. The dissonance is real][1]

“What we’re really scared of is that the people in power have not got our back and they don’t give a shit about whether we survive or not.”

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there is no commentary on it other than any minimal notes I may have included. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some/more of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not. Should the article be behind a paywall let me know and I will figure out a way to get it to you.[1]: https://www.theguardian.com/wellness/ng-interactive/2025/may/22/hypernormalization-dysfunction-status-quo

item.title: Harvard says no chance it will comply with changes feds demand
item.description:
Check out this article: [Harvard says no chance it will comply with changes feds demand][1]

Good for them: The university will not surrender its independence or relinquish its constitutional rights. Neither Harvard nor any other private university can allow itself to be taken over by the federal government. Accordingly, Harvard will not accept the government’s terms as an agreement in principle.

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there is no commentary on it other than any minimal notes I may have included. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some/more of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not. Should the article be behind a paywall let me know and I will figure out a way to get it to you.[1]: https://arstechnica.com/science/2025/04/harvard-says-feds-research-funding-demands-amount-to-a-takeover/

item.title: ESPN’s Pat McAfee and others amplified a false rumor. A teenager’s life was ‘destroyed’
item.description:
Check out this article: [ESPN’s Pat McAfee and others amplified a false rumor. A teenager’s life was ‘destroyed’][1]

I seriously hope her lawsuits payoff. The irresponsibility and crassness of talking heads and influencers is disgustingly.

“I would like people to be held accountable for what they’ve done,” she said. “You’re ruining my life by talking about it on your show for nothing but attention, but here I am staying up until 5 in the morning, every night, throwing up, not eating because I’m so anxious about what’s going to happen for the rest of my life.”

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there is no commentary on it other than any minimal notes I may have included. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some/more of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not. Should the article be behind a paywall let me know and I will figure out a way to get it to you.[1]: https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/6245376/2025/04/01/pat-mcafee-espn-ole-miss-student/?source=user_shared_article

item.title: This Is Making Us Sick… Here’s How We Fix It
item.description:
Check out this article: [This Is Making Us Sick… Here’s How We Fix It][1]

As someone who creates technical solutions for a living I still think there’s a lot of truth here. Life isn’t an either/or choice, but rather yes/and. Use the tech to enhance and augment your life, not to replace it.

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there is no commentary on it other than any minimal notes I may have included. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some/more of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not. Should the article be behind a paywall let me know and I will figure out a way to get it to you.[1]: https://youtube.com/watch?v=pzQ3IN6aiO8&si=iE7oOOl1sb2iilFI

item.title: In the 1990s, They Were So Bad People Didn’t Want Them for Free. Now They’re a Miracle. Can America Catch Up?
item.description:
Check out this article: [In the 1990s, They Were So Bad People Didn’t Want Them for Free. Now They’re a Miracle. Can America Catch Up?][1]

The president has said he’s focused on making things more affordable for families, Yet rolling back standards that reduce energy and water use and bills isn’t going to save people money. It’s just the opposite

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there is no commentary on it other than any minimal notes I may have included. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some/more of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not. Should the article be behind a paywall let me know and I will figure out a way to get it to you.[1]: https://slate.com/technology/2025/02/toilets-low-flow-trump-environment-cost-savings.html

item.title: Ok, but who should you pick?
item.description:
Check out this article: Ok, but who should you pick?

Stop it with the “we’re not naming names”. I’m so sick of companies pulling that shit because they’re worried about DT retaliating. The man is unhinged, and a terrible person. It sickens me that even close to 1/2 of the country sees no problem with the ugly, petty, vindictive, divisive, dangerous platform he represents.

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there is no commentary on it other than any minimal notes I may have included. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some/more of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not. Should the article be behind a paywall let me know and I will figure out a way to get it to you.

item.title: Chicken Littles Are Ruining America
item.description:
Check out this article: Chicken Littles Are Ruining America

“We have produced a culture that celebrates catastrophizing… Trump thrives in an atmosphere of menace. Authoritarianism flourishes amid pessimism, fear, and rage. Trump feeds off zero-sum thinking, the notion that society is war—us-versus-them, dog-eat-dog. The more you contribute to the culture of depressive negativity, the more likely Trump’s reelection becomes.”

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there is no commentary on it other than any minimal notes I may have included. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some/more of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not. Should the article be behind a paywall let me know and I will figure out a way to get it to you.

item.title: Your iPhone will soon be able to replicate your voice after 15 minutes of training

item.title: Exclusive: Elon Musk orders removal of Twitter suicide prevention feature, sources say
item.description:
The last straw right here and why I will never go back. Don’t believe the BS they spew that they just took it down to improve it. Something like that can be worked in parallel, and to remove it at this time of year is nearly criminal IMO.

Check out this article: Exclusive: Elon Musk orders removal of Twitter suicide prevention feature, sources say.

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there’s no commentary on it. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not.

item.title: Everything You Learned About Thanksgiving Is Wrong
item.description:
Check out this article: Everything You Learned About Thanksgiving Is Wrong.

No, this doesn’t mean I’m some far left lib and think we have to quit doing Thanksgiving, but I do think we should at least not pretend things weren’t as they were. And even then, realize that the ideals we are (hopefully) striving for: gratitude, community, family, and togetherness, do matter.

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there’s no commentary on it. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not.

item.title: Why Does the U.S. Have So Many Mass Shootings? Research Is Clear: Guns.
item.description:
Check out this article: Why Does the U.S. Have So Many Mass Shootings? Research Is Clear: Guns..

Can’t wait to see how many people make the assumption I think all guns should be confiscated purely based on the article or its title. I don’t. I do think we have way to many guns in this country, and too lax of laws around them (can you understand the distinction?). And anytime there is something that can used for a deadly purpose without ￼adequate controls and restricted access there is a higher potential for that thing to be used in the heat of the moment (on oneself or others). Hopes and prayers will never bring anyone back or stop someone from violence, and it’s time to stop pretending like they will.

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there’s no commentary on it. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not.

item.title: ‘Dying Inside’: Chaos and Cruelty in Louisiana Juvenile Detention
item.description:
Check out this article: ‘Dying Inside’: Chaos and Cruelty in Louisiana Juvenile Detention.

One of the saddest things I find in this article is the number of families at the point they think the experience of jail will make their kids “scared straight”, or that the kid just doesn’t have a responsible adult in their life. Instead of “scared straight” we get “traumatized into submission” and sometimes it’s the ultimate nihilist submission… suicide. Incarceration (esp the way and frequency we do it) is not the answer. Even more so for kids.

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there’s no commentary on it. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not.

item.title: Your words are wasted
item.description:

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there’s no commentary on it. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not.

item.title: Elon Musk demands Twitter employees commit to ‘extremely hardcore’ culture or leave
item.description:
Check out this article: Elon Musk demands Twitter employees commit to ‘extremely hardcore’ culture or leave.

F that Sh*+. Unfortunately he’s going to have people that have to have the job for immigration, health, or financial purposes. And we’ll hold it up as a badge of honor to celebrate hustle culture.

Hint, if you’re an employee that isn’t compensated proportionally (and that could be negatively or positively) given the company’s performance there is absolutely nothing worth grinding for in a JOB.

Note: Since this is an automatically created post there’s no commentary on it. Read it and do your own critical thinking. I may get around to adding some of my own thoughts or categorizing it… but maybe not.

item.title: 100 Rules — Personal Philosophy
item.description:

item.title: I could sure use a coffee
Backseat driver
What to read
item.description:
Writer Doris Lessing on how to choose what to read:

“There is only one way to read, which is to browse in libraries and bookshops, picking up books that attract you, reading only those, dropping them when they bore you, skipping the parts that drag – and never, never reading anything because you feel you ought, or because it is part of a trend or a movement. Remember that the book which bores you when you are twenty or thirty will open doors for you when you are forty or fifty-and vise versa. Don’t read a book out of its right time for you.”

item.title: Real Beauty
Like a Girl
Marc Andreessen’s Guide to Personal Productivity
item.description:
Yes, it’s an older post, but still some interesting ideas. Like he says, they may not be for everyone (including himself), but there may be something of use. I may even try the index card thing…

[https://pmarchive.com/guide_to_personal_productivity.html][1][1]: https://pmarchive.com/guide_to_personal_productivity.html

item.title: The Motivation Manifesto
item.description:
Our challenge is that we have been conditioned to believe the opposite of these things – that bold action or swift progress is somehow dangerous or reckless. But a certain degree of insanity and recklessness is necessary to advance or innovate anything, to make any new or remarkable or meaningful contributions. What great thing was ever accomplished without a little recklessness? So-called recklessness was required for the extraordinary to happen: crossing the oceans, ending slavery, rocketing man into space, building skyscrapers, decoding the genome, starting new businesses, and innovating entire industries. It is reckless to try something that has never been done, to move against convention, to begin before all conditions are good and preparations are perfected. But the bold know that to win, one must first begin. They also deeply understand that a degree of risk is inevitable and necessary should there be any real reward. Yes, any plunge into the unknown is reckless – but that’s where the treasure lies.

From [The Motivation Manifesto][1][1]: https://markjacobsen.net/book/motivation-manifesto

item.title: Podcasts Archive – The ONE Thing
item.description:
A good refresher on being clear about your priorities.
Podcasts Archive – The ONE Thing

item.title: Kwik Brain 045: Improve Your Memory With A Good Night’s Sleep With Dr. Michael Breus | Jim Kwik
item.description:
A great reminder. Love the 5 step process…

1 Keep 1 sleep schedule (wake up the same time every day)
2 Stop caffeine before 2pm
3 Stop alcohol intake 3 hours before bed
4 Stop exercise 4 hours before bed
5 Give the sun a high five – 5 minutes of direct sun

Kwik Brain 045: Improve Your Memory With A Good Night’s Sleep With Dr. Michael Breus | Jim Kwik

item.title: Did a U.S. Veteran Influence Kaepernick’s ‘Take a Knee’ Protest of Police Brutality?
item.description:

item.title: Weekly Challenge 1: Nothing Alaming
item.description:
Welcome to the first in a series of [weekly challenges][1]. This first one is “nothing alarming”, and anyone should be able to do it.

Simply pick a time you’re comfortable waking up. Set an alarm, and wake up when it goes off.

What’s the challenge here? For some it may be waking up to an alarm. But here’s the kicker… No using the “snooze” and you have to wake up at the same time on the weekend (and tomorrow if you have it off).

To join the challenge search in coach.me for “WC01” or go to [my goal page][2] where you can find it and join in. Each day when you wake up to your alarm, be sure to “check in” on [coach.me][3].[1]: http://markjacobsen.net/2016/02/join-my-weekly-challenge/ [2]: https://www.coach.me/users/a6d2ab7f4e8a70753896/goals [3]: http://markjacobsen.net/apps/coachme/

item.title: Photo Journal for 2015-02-24
item.description: Rest in Peace Grandpa. You are always in our hearts and on our minds.

item.title: charity:water update
item.description:
Wow. Just the other day I received an updated report on what my 2012 birthday campaign helped achieve and I have to say I’m absolutely floored. The $1001 we raised helped over 860 people!

Let me say that again, you helped over 860 people.

There’s a reason I’ve always been a big fan of charity:water and these types of results just help to reinforce that belief. Take a look at the projects here: Project Page

Wow. Just wow. And THANK YOU!!!

item.title: Missing You
item.description:
I sit here on the eve of your death some 15 years ago. Of course I’ve had a few and have been listening to Runaway Train and Crazy Train a number of times. Not even sure why I’m writing this, but of course I do. It’s because I miss you. My girls miss you. And they never met you. You eliminated that possibility.

For years I’ve tried to come to terms with why you did what you did. I still can’t say I understand. I can’t say that I’ll ever be able to let it go.

Try figuring out how to explain suicide to a brilliant 6 year old, or a 4 year old that won’t stop asking questions. Sure I’ve read the articles. The thing is, kids aren’t dumb. So instead we just stick with the fact that Uncle Scott died. We already have to deal with tears just for that fact. Often for no apparent reason.

Why not just write this in a “journal”? Because the thing is that in the “adult world” we like to gloss over the issue. We make gun to the head gestures in jest not knowing the potential pain we’re bringing to those nearby. We take the “righteous” and “pious” positions that suicide is a sin or that the person who committed suicide was “weak” or “cowardly”.

Maybe, but what gives you the right? WHAT GIVES YOU THE RIGHT???

The thing is, suicide takes someone from a family. From loved ones. From people who care about them.

Even if they’ve screwed up. Even if they’ve done stupid things. Even if [insert your own reason].

The point is… I would argue that mental illness is a real thing. It’s not a weakness unless by weakness you mean a deficiency in some area that most of us have plenty of reserves (or at lease enough to make it through).

To wrap up this rant let me just say…

  • I apologize to any coworkers
  • I’m sorry Mom and Dad (let me know if you want me to delete this. I may anyway)
  • Even if my girls never see this they mean more to me than words can ever describe and it makes me appreciate how far my parents have come, because I’m not sure I could be so strong.
  • I miss you Scott

Anyway, I’ll close with the poignant words to Runaway Train…

Promised myself I wouldn’t weep. One more promise I couldn’t keep. It seems no one can help me now. I’m in too deep there’s no way out…

Can you help me remember how to smile? Make it somehow all seem worthwhile? How on earth did I get so jaded? …

It seems no one can help me now. I’m in too deep there’s no way out. This time I have really led myself astray.

Bought a ticket on a runaway train. Like a mad man laughing at the rain. Little out of touch. Little insane. Just easier than dealing with the pain.

item.title: Let me help you buy or sell that house!
item.description:
It may have been a little quiet around here lately, but that’s because I’ve been hard at work studying for my real estate sales license. Fortunately, I passed the exam on Thursday and am now free to help anyone buy or sell real estate in the state of Michigan!

Visit my new site at FreedomRE.com (note it’s still under some construction)

Why did I want to get my license? It’s actually been something I’ve been interested in for a long time, and briefly considered probably 6 or 7 years ago. With a strong interest and actual background in finances, technology, business, and sales I figured that should pair well with my interest in Real Estate. In addition, I’ve been selling real estate Call Capture Hotlines to real estate agents and mortgage brokers for the last 9 years.

What differentiates me and why would someone want to work with me? I would say it’s a combination of personality fit, professionalism, and competence. As anyone who has worked with me before knows, I have a reputation for getting things done and putting my client first. I’m not going to gush all over you or sit and gab your ear off, but I will provide you my 12+ years of technology and finance experience, 10+ years sales and marketing experience, and my reputation for success. Most of all, I don’t think you’ll find another agent in the area with anywhere close to my background and skills.

I would love a chance to put my money where my mouth is and help you buy or sell that home! Contact me today:

mark@freedomre.com
517-252-4440
http://www.freedomre.com/contact

item.title: The Single Easiest Habit Change to Improve Your Life Forever #1aDay
item.description: This one thing has made a huge difference in my life. Find out about it by reading the full article here

item.title: Happy birthday America
item.description:
As I sit here watching “The Patriot” I can’t help but be appreciative of all those who have sacrificed for the dream and reality that is America. Those who gave the ultimate sacrifice and those who left their families – if even for a short time – in service of a cause they considered greater than themselves.

In salute of all the Patriots: Abe Lincoln, Alexander Hamilton, George Washington, John Adams, Sam Adams, Ben Franklin, Teddy Roosevelt, George Patton, General Sherman, General Grant, FDR, those who will never be known (the unknown soldiers), and many many more I am ever grateful to have been born in this wonderful Land of Opportunity.

As a parent, I haven’t the luxury of principles. I have long feared that my sins will come back to haunt me – and the weight is more than I can bare.

Thank you again to all who have sacrificed so that we may pursue happiness.

item.title: Thankful for Being Blessed
item.description:
As we enter the stereotypical season of thanks I’ve been looking back at things that we’ve accomplished over the years, and I’ve realized that I have been extremely blessed.  I’m blessed…

  • To have been born in America
  • To be healthy
  • To have parents from a working class background
  • To have had a good education
  • To have married a wonderful woman
  • To have healthy children
  • To have a great company
  • To have a great job
  • To have been taught: discipline, self-sacrifice, and hard work

For any of you that helped to heap these many blessings on me, I say “Thank You!” now, tomorrow, and forever.
